title: Isolate one variable per ablation
description: Each ablation changes exactly one factor so the score delta is attributable to that factor, not a confound.
body: Use when measuring what a change contributes. Do not change the prompt, model, and retrieval together and credit the gain to one, and avoid comparing across different datasets or judge versions. Actions: hold everything fixed except the one factor under test, keep the dataset, metric, and judge constant, and record the exact configuration of each arm. Validation signals include a recorded config diff showing a single changed variable between arms. Risk boundary: a multi-variable change makes the result uninterpretable and can ship a regression hidden by an unrelated gain; isolate variables or label the result inconclusive.
